subject: Computer Networking
title: Antenna Types and Patterns
description: A 60-second lesson on Antenna Types and Patterns.

hook.text: An antenna doesn't create energy. It focuses it. The shape determines where the signal goes.

buildup.text: Omnidirectional antennas radiate in all directions like a donut. Used for general indoor coverage.

discovery.text: Directional antennas focus signal one way like a flashlight. Used for point-to-point long-range links.

twist.text: MIMO uses multiple antennas at once. Each sends a different data stream, multiplying throughput.

climax.text: Beamforming adjusts antenna patterns dynamically, focusing energy toward each client for better range.

punchline.text: The antenna shapes the signal. The right one changes everything.

quiz.question: What does beamforming do?
options: Dynamically focuses the wireless signal toward each client
Blocks signals from reaching outside the building
Converts wireless signals to fiber optic light
